Finance & Economy.headline: Wall Street edges higher as AI rally offsets geopolitical concerns
Finance & Economy.detail: US stocks rose slightly, with AI-driven gains outweighing concerns over stalled US-Iran talks and rising oil prices; the S&P 500 and Nasdaq recorded fresh all-time closing highs.

Finance & Economy.headline: US adds 115,000 jobs despite AI and war concerns, unemployment stays at 4.3%
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite concerns surrounding AI and the ongoing war, the US economy added 115,000 jobs, maintaining a steady unemployment rate of 4.3%.

Finance & Economy.headline: US-Iran ceasefire talks collapsed; US stocks stayed pretty high thanks to chipmaker surge
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite collapsed ceasefire talks between the U.S. and Iran, and Trump's rejection of Tehran's offer, U.S. stocks remained high, driven by a surge in chipmakers.

Finance & Economy.headline: Microvast Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
Finance & Economy.detail: Microvast reported a net profit per share of $0.15 for Q1 2026, compared to $0.19 in Q1 2025, with revenue decreasing by 48.0% to $60.6 million due to evolving regulatory and geopolitical dynamics.

Finance & Economy.headline: Will our capital gains tax be an economy and market killer?
Finance & Economy.detail: Changes to the capital gains tax (CGT) discount could make Australia among the highest taxers of capital gains, potentially impacting small businesses, venture capital investors, and start-ups.

Technology.headline: Google Spots Hackers Using AI To Find Zero-Day Flaw For Mass Exploitation
Technology.detail: Google disrupted a criminal group's attempt to use artificial intelligence to exploit another company's previously unknown digital vulnerability, highlighting growing cybersecurity concerns around AI.

Technology.headline: Tesla's Latest Recall Aims to Prevent the Cybertruck's Wheels From Flying Off
Technology.detail: Tesla is recalling Cybertrucks due to a potential issue where the wheel studs could separate from the wheel hub, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA).

Technology.headline: Prime Video Becomes Latest Streamer to Add Vertical Feed for Content Discovery
Technology.detail: Amazon's Prime Video has introduced a vertical 'Clips' feed, similar to TikTok, which is tailored to user interests and updated based on viewing history to enhance content discovery.

Technology.headline: Microsoft exec Shawn Bice returns to AWS to lead reliability push for AI agents
Technology.detail: Shawn Bice, a Microsoft executive, is returning to Amazon Web Services (AWS) to spearhead a reliability initiative focused on AI agents.

Technology.headline: Apple Hedges Bets on Chips in Talks with Intel, Samsung
Technology.detail: Apple is reportedly considering partnerships with Intel and Samsung for U.S.-based chip production, aiming to diversify its manufacturing locations beyond Taiwan due to geopolitical and supply chain concerns.

Science.headline: The ugliest animal on Earth may be humanity's best hope for living longer — here's the science
Science.detail: Naked mole rats, known for their longevity and cancer resistance, possess high levels of a protective molecule called HMW-HA; researchers transferred a gene responsible for HMW-HA production into mice, leading to increased lifespan and reduced inflammation, suggesting potential for improving human health and aging.

Science.headline: CIMMYT Receives 2025 Al-Sumait Prize for African Development, Recognizing Decades of Science for Food Security
Science.detail: The Kuwait Fund for the Advancement of Sciences has named CIMMYT as the recipient of the 2025 Al-Sumait Prize for African Development, which recognizes the organization's science-driven contributions to food security, agricultural resilience, and the livelihoods of millions of smallholder farmers across the continent.

Science.headline: Ways Sanger is helping to transform women's health
Science.detail: A new study reveals how the maternal immune system is modified early in pregnancy, potentially helping researchers understand miscarriages and preeclampsia by showing how cells from the developing placenta interact with the mother's immune system.

Science.headline: Two decades of immune insights leads to a bold collaboration
Science.detail: Professor Phil Hodgkin and Professor Ken Duffy are teaming up to unlock the keys to immune health, using mathematical expertise to support Hodgkin's team and help them achieve their vision.

Science.headline: Research manipulation mapped in new Forensic Scientometrics report
Science.detail: The Forensic Scientometrics (FoSci) movement has released its first report, uniting experts to address manipulation of global research, covering topics like data manipulation, authorship fraud, and the role of AI in enabling misconduct.

Health.headline: This 800-year-old Chinese Exercise Helps Lower Blood Pressure Naturally
Health.detail: An ancient Chinese exercise routine may be just as powerful as a daily brisk walk for lowering blood pressure, without equipment, gyms, or intense workouts. Adults with stage 1 hypertension who practiced baduanjin showed significant benefits in a major clinical trial.

Health.headline: Scientists Say 8,500 Steps a Day Could Stop Weight from Creeping Back
Health.detail: A new international analysis suggests there may be a surprisingly simple secret to keeping weight off after dieting: walking about 8,500 steps a day. Researchers found that people who boosted their daily steps to around that level during a diet maintained their weight loss.

Health.headline: Ultra-processed Foods Linked to Higher Risk of Heart Disease and Early Death
Health.detail: A major new European cardiology report warns that people who eat the most ultra-processed foods face significantly higher risks of heart disease and early death. Ultra-processed foods may be doing far more damage than many people realize.

Health.headline: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers enter isolation facility after evacuation to UK
Health.detail: Passengers from a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak have been evacuated to the UK and placed in an isolation facility. The evacuation and isolation are part of efforts to control the spread of the virus.

Health.headline: FDA Approves Ocrelizumab for Pediatric Relapsing MS
Health.detail: The FDA has approved Ocrelizumab for the treatment of pediatric relapsing multiple sclerosis. This approval marks a significant advancement in treatment options for young patients with this condition.

Sports.headline: Taiwan's shooters win World Cup gold in Kazakhstan
Sports.detail: Taiwan's Yang Kun-pi and Liu Wan-yu won gold in the trap mixed team event at the World Cup Shotgun in Kazakhstan, setting a new world record with hits on 36 targets. The Taiwanese duo dominated the final, hitting their first 10 shots and scoring a world record 36 combined hits to defeat the rival teams from Italy, India and China.

Sports.headline: PBA: Japeth Aguilar returns for Ginebra ahead of playoffs
Sports.detail: Japeth Aguilar is set to return for Ginebra in time for the playoffs, boosting the team's chances. The veteran player's comeback is expected to provide a significant lift to the team's campaign.

Sports.headline: Missouri football star Ahmad Hardy in stable condition after being shot at concert
Sports.detail: Missouri football star Ahmad Hardy is in stable condition after being shot at a concert. The incident has raised concerns about the safety of athletes and the prevalence of gun violence.

Sports.headline: Astros' Teng Kai-wei takes loss in start against Reds
Sports.detail: Teng Kai-wei, the only active Taiwanese pitcher in MLB, suffered his third loss of the season against the Cincinnati Reds. He allowed three earned runs over three innings, raising his ERA to 3.12.

Sports.headline: Lucas Herbert caps off wire-to-wire win at LIV Golf Virginia
Sports.detail: Lucas Herbert secured a wire-to-wire victory at the LIV Golf Virginia event, showcasing a dominant performance. This win marks a significant achievement in his career and adds to the excitement of the LIV Golf season.

Entertainment.headline: Savannah Guthrie to present Wordle TV show
Entertainment.detail: Savannah Guthrie is set to host a TV show based on the popular word game Wordle.

Entertainment.headline: Sienna Miller welcomes third child
Entertainment.detail: Actress Sienna Miller has recently given birth to her third child.

Entertainment.headline: Westlife aim for chart milestone with career spanning Ultimate Collection
Entertainment.detail: The Irish pop group Westlife is releasing an 'Ultimate Collection' album, hoping to achieve a significant chart milestone.

Entertainment.headline: George and Amal Clooney lead the best-dressed at star-studded King's Trust 50th anniversary
Entertainment.detail: George and Amal Clooney were highlighted as being among the best-dressed attendees at the King's Trust 50th-anniversary celebration.

Entertainment.headline: Shannon Elizabeth has finalized her divorce from second husband Simon Borchert amid her OnlyFans rise.
Entertainment.detail: Shannon Elizabeth's divorce from Simon Borchert is finalized as she gains prominence on OnlyFans, marking a new chapter after ending her marriage.

Crime & Justice.headline: More than 25 Defendants Charged in Nationwide Tren de Aragua Crackdown Resulting in the Seizure of Over 80 Firearms and Narcotics in Homeland Security Task Force Investigations
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department announced charges against more than 25 defendants following a nationwide crackdown on the Tren de Aragua gang, resulting in the seizure of over 80 firearms and narcotics.

Crime & Justice.headline: Extra jail time for 'kill cars', recruiting kids for crime in NSW
Crime & Justice.detail: Criminals in New South Wales, Australia, who recruit children for crimes or use 'kill cars' could face increased jail time, as the government introduces legislation to create new offenses and tighten bail conditions for those with gang links. Anyone who sets a car alight after using it to traffic drugs, supply firearms or commit other serious crimes could face up to 12 years in prison.

Crime & Justice.headline: 2 injured after gunman opens fire on Memorial Drive in Cambridge, authorities say
Crime & Justice.detail: A gunman opened fire at cars on Memorial Drive in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and was shot by a responding State Police trooper and a civilian, resulting in injuries to both the gunman and the civilian.

Crime & Justice.headline: Family of Florida university shooting victim sues over suspect's ChatGPT use
Crime & Justice.detail: The family of a victim in last year's deadly mass shooting at Florida State University is suing ChatGPT developer OpenAI, accusing them of enabling the suspect leading up to the attack. The lawsuit claims the shooter used ChatGPT for assistance in planning and carrying out the shooting.

Crime & Justice.headline: U.S. Department of Justice Announces Compensation Process for Victims of AirBit Club Fraud Scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: The U.S. Department of Justice has announced a compensation process for the victims of the AirBit Club fraud scheme.

Education.headline: Pacific Oaks College to Host 2026 Commencement Ceremony at the Pasadena Civic Auditorium
Education.detail: Pacific Oaks College will celebrate the graduation of 421 students at its annual commencement ceremony on May 16, 2026, recognizing graduates from various programs in education, social work, and related fields who are entering a workforce facing critical shortages.

Education.headline: Preserving the soul of higher education
Education.detail: The Commission on Higher Education (CHED) in the Philippines is facing criticism for a proposal to dilute the General Education (GE) curriculum, with concerns raised that it could undermine critical thinking and ethical citizenship among students, and that academic efficiency should not come at the expense of intellectual depth and civic formation.

Education.headline: Taiwan tertiary enrollment drops by nearly 22 percent over 14 years
Education.detail: Taiwan's tertiary student population has fallen to 1.057 million in the 2025-2026 academic year, a decline of about 295,000, or 21.8 percent, over the past 14 years, according to Ministry of Education (MOE) data, with undergraduate enrollment driving the decline.

Education.headline: Fees-free: Scheme failed to get more students into tertiary education - Govt
Education.detail: The New Zealand Government decided to scrap the fees-free tertiary education scheme due to its failure to boost enrolments, poor uptake among disadvantaged students, and ongoing fiscal pressure; the policy initially covered the first year of study but was changed to cover the final year.

Education.headline: Emerging Futures 9: visa uncertainty reshapes student demand globally
Education.detail: More international students are ruling out study destinations earlier because of visa uncertainty, costs and a lack of clarity about what an application will involve, according to new global research from IDP Education.

Lifestyle.headline: Experts wonder 'Where is the CDC?' as a hantavirus outbreak unfolds on a cruise ship.
Lifestyle.detail: Health experts are questioning the CDC's role as a hantavirus outbreak occurs on a cruise, while a pediatrics group has issued new guidance on recess for the first time in 13 years, and advice spreads on social media.

Lifestyle.headline: Ram Charan's Trainer Calls Him Salman's Replica, Reveals Peddi Fitness Routine
Lifestyle.detail: Ram Charan's trainer discusses his fitness routine, likening him to Salman Khan.

Lifestyle.headline: What Happens When You Drink Green Tea Every Day For 2 Weeks
Lifestyle.detail: An article discusses the potential effects of consuming green tea daily for a two-week period.

Lifestyle.headline: Big Smile, No Teeth: Did you remember to pay the water bill last month?
Lifestyle.detail: Jason Godfrey discusses financial habits and how they might relate to the early detection of dementia, highlighting a study that showed money management skills deteriorate before other cognitive functions.

Lifestyle.headline: The dark side of fast fashion recycling
Lifestyle.detail: A report reveals the hidden human and environmental costs of the fast fashion recycling industry in India, where discarded clothing from around the world is processed, exposing workers to hazardous chemicals and polluting the environment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: RightsCon nixing is just the start
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The cancelation of RightsCon 2026 in Zambia was not merely a logistical setback, but a political decision with global consequences, concerning anyone who cares about open international dialogue and digital rights.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Taiwan needs a silicon network
Opinion/Editorial.detail: If the meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping this week is framed only in terms of clashes over tariffs and Taiwan's place on the negotiating table, it would be a misunderstanding of the nature of great power exchanges.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Labour's rebellion: Starmer faces a crisis of legitimacy
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses Keir Starmer's challenges in maintaining legitimacy within the Labour party.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on World Cup ticket prices: $33,000? You're having a laugh…
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial expresses outrage over exorbitant ticket prices for the World Cup.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Sacramento would rather go after independent journalists than fraud
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This Washington Post editorial criticizes Sacramento's focus on targeting independent journalists instead of addressing fraud.

World.headline: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire is 'On Life Support' as Hopes for a Deal Fade
World.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the ceasefire with Iran is barely holding after Iran's response to a U.S. proposal revealed significant disagreements between the two nations, with Iran demanding an end to the naval blockade and a guarantee of no further attacks. Tehran has also emphasized its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial route for global oil and gas supply.

World.headline: EU Agrees Sanctions on Israeli West Bank Settlers, Hamas Leaders
World.detail: EU foreign ministers have approved sanctions on Israeli settlers involved in violence against Palestinians in the West Bank, as well as against Hamas leaders. This decision comes amidst increasing concerns over settler violence and settlement expansion in the occupied territories.

World.headline: Chinese President to Discuss US Arms Sales to Taiwan with Trump
World.detail: During President Trump's upcoming state visit to China, Chinese President Xi Jinping intends to discuss the United States' arms sales to Taiwan. China recognizes only Beijing but the US is required to provide weapons to Taiwan so that it can defend itself.

World.headline: Hantavirus-hit Ship Sets Sail for Netherlands as Final Passengers Evacuated in Tenerife
World.detail: The MV Hondius, affected by a hantavirus outbreak, has departed Tenerife for the Netherlands after the final passengers were evacuated. Preliminary tests confirmed that one of the Spanish evacuees tested positive for the virus.

World.headline: U.K.'s Starmer Under Renewed Pressure as Lawmakers Urge Him to Quit
World.detail: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is facing increased pressure as four ministerial aides resigned and over 70 Labour lawmakers publicly called for his resignation following poor results in local elections. Cabinet ministers have reportedly suggested he set a timeline for his departure to ensure an orderly transition.

Zimbabwe.headline: Human remains found at Silobela mine
Zimbabwe.detail: Human remains were discovered at a mine in Silobela.

Zimbabwe.headline: MP hands over solar-powered boreholes amid Byo water crisis
Zimbabwe.detail: A Member of Parliament has provided solar-powered boreholes to address the water crisis in Bulawayo.

Zimbabwe.headline: Doctors accuse medical aid societies of running “Ponzi Scheme,”
Zimbabwe.detail: Doctors are alleging that medical aid societies are operating like a Ponzi scheme.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe and China's zero-tariff era: Turning opportunity into national prosperity
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe and China's zero-tariff policy is seen as an opportunity for national economic prosperity.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe reclaims steel crown . . . US$500m investment for Manhize Steel Plant
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is reportedly reclaiming its position in the steel industry with a US$500 million investment in the Manhize Steel Plant.
